name: Raisin Sauce for Smoked Ham and Tongue
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine the brown sugar, dry mustard, and flour.
2. Mix well.
3. Gradually add the seedless raisins, vinegar, and water to the mixture.
4. Stir continuously until all ingredients are fully incorporated.
5. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens into a syrup consistency.
This is the classic raisin sauce. It's very simple yet delicious! Many have used this recipe for decades, from the 1930s to the 1960s, both professionally and at home. The recipe has always received only compliments.
